text.title: Scope Management Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Verbal scope agreements without written scope statement sign-off.
antipattern.root-cause: Undocumented promises are interpreted differently by each party.
antipattern.fix: Require written, signed scope statement; verbal agreement does not bind.

antipattern.symptom: Vague scope language: 'make it better', 'improve performance'.
antipattern.root-cause: Cannot be accepted, rejected, or measured.
antipattern.fix: Every AC must have a numeric or observable condition.

antipattern.symptom: Agent regenerates requirements from scratch on every pass; IDs and wording drift.
antipattern.fix: Freeze canonical text; force unified-diff updates only.

antipattern.symptom: Agent invents non-functional requirements without explicit named source.
antipattern.fix: Cap NFR generation to requirements with explicit named source; reject agent-invented NFRs.
